translated: Abraham said, indeed, that he would cut down the trees, that he would gather the fruits of the forest, because the people were suffering greatly, and he would build a house for them, to live in peace and abundance.

translated: The people of Dindaan are gathering with their traditions.

text: Ní u gíti cáá dipool ki màl̄ kibininjabiki, ní ki bí yii: «Ti-Dindaan nín ká yii m-cal kaa néeń-mee ní u gíti pú-mi kibininjabiki kiba.» Ní u pú-kì diyindi yii Simeeyɔn, taapu di sá yii: <Ti-Dindaan gbìl̄.>
translated: And he went to the pool where the people were gathering, and he said to them: 'Ti-Dindaan, you who are saying "m-cal kaa neen-mee," and you who come to the pool to gather, come.' Then he turned to Simeeyon and said to him: 'Ti-Dindaan has departed.'

text: Kutaa wúntèe ní ukpil tùkū u-naal yii: «M fu dōoǹ ti-baa cee kunyeeu. Tí gíti nyùntī-u ndaam kunyeeu nee si mun ń cùú dòon̄ u-cee, tí ŋmā kí tīī ti-baa inibool.»
translated: "I don't want to go and see you. Because you know the way to come to see you, so that the people can come and see you, now that you know, go to the meeting."
